name: BackstopJS
description: Open-source visual regression testing tool that runs Headless Chrome, Puppeteer or Playwright to compare screenshots against baselines.

seo.title: BackstopJS — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: BackstopJS: Open-source visual regression testing tool that runs Headless Chrome, Puppeteer or Playwright to compare screenshots against baselines.

when_to_use: Catch CSS regressions with automated screenshot diffing
Visual regression CI gate on every pull request
Multi-viewport baseline comparisons for responsive UI
Approve UI changes via visual diff review workflow

pros: Free, self-hosted visual regression with headless Chrome
Config-driven, easy to add to existing CI pipelines
Built-in HTML reporter with diff viewer
cons: Flaky on sites with ads, animations or rolling dates
No baseline storage service, bring your own git or S3
Slower than hosted tools like Percy or Chromatic
review.verdict: Good free starting point for visual regression, but expect to babysit flakes and storage yourself.
review.best_for: Small teams wanting self-hosted visual diffing without SaaS fees
review.not_for: Teams needing parallel cloud runs, review workflows or cross-browser matrices
